metadata.title: Unit Mismatch — Mars Climate Orbiter ($325M)
metadata.note: CLI video: B00 intro · B01 PROBLEM · B02 ASK · B03 CODE · B04 OUTPUT(manim) · B05 CHANGE · B06–B08 slates · B09 outro

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI, then take it apart.

beats.role_note: the problem, the context, the stakes — BEFORE the CLI
beats.narration_text: In 1999 NASA lost a three-hundred-twenty-five-million-dollar spacecraft because one engineering team used pound-force-seconds and another expected newton-seconds. The conversion factor — one pound-force equals four-point-four-four-eight newtons — was in every reference table. Nobody checked the label. This video builds the type system that would have caught it.
beats.visual_intent: title card: 'Mars Climate Orbiter — $325M lost to a missing unit label · 1 lbf = 4.44822 N'; no terminal yet

beats.narration_text: Ask Claude to build the validator. Define a DimensionalValue class with value and unit. Implement add that raises UnitMismatchError if units are incompatible and auto-converts if compatible. Define a unit conversion table for force, length, mass, and time. Run the Mars Climate Orbiter scenario: thruster force in pound-force-seconds passed to a system expecting newton-seconds. Catch the mismatch. Print a validation report.

props.command: claude "Write a Python script: define DimensionalValue class with value and unit. Implement add() that raises UnitMismatchError if units incompatible; auto-converts if compatible. Define unit conversion table for force (N, lbf), length (m, km, ft), mass (kg, lb), time (s, min). Run Mars Climate Orbiter scenario: thruster force in lbf*s passed to system expecting N*s — catch the mismatch. Print validation report."

beats.narration_text: Read the conversion table — conversion underscore factors: lbf to N equals four-point-four-four-eight-two-two, ft to m equals zero-point-three-zero-four-eight, and so on. The NASA orbiter transmitted eighteen-point-nine pound-force-seconds. The receiving system expected the value in newton-seconds — it got the number without the unit, multiplied by a factor of four-point-four.

Draw the pipeline. The thruster transmits eighteen-point-nine pound-force-seconds. It hits the interface gate — unit mismatch, the gate fires red. The navigation system receives the value anyway, interpreting it as newton-seconds — off by a factor of four-point-four. The trajectory diverges from the expected path. The orbiter enters the Martian atmosphere at the wrong angle.
beats.visual_intent: Manim: three-node pipeline (Thruster → Interface Gate → Navigation); crimson mismatch gate; diverging trajectory lines below showing expected vs actual path

beats.narration_text: Now fix it. Iterate: update the validator to auto-convert compatible units — if lbf-seconds arrives where N-seconds is expected, apply the conversion and log a warning. Re-run the Mars scenario and show the corrected value alongside the conversion audit trail.

props.command: claude "update unit_validator.py: add auto-convert for compatible units. If lbf*s received where N*s expected, convert and log WARNING with the factor applied. Re-run Mars scenario: thruster sends 18.9 lbf*s -> validator converts to 84.0 N*s -> navigation receives correct value. Print audit trail."

beats.role_note: slate — fill with screen recording or Manim
beats.narration_text: Eighteen-point-nine pound-force-seconds times four-point-four-four-eight equals eighty-four-point-zero newton-seconds. The validator logs the conversion, the navigation system receives the correct value, and the warning is in the audit trail. A labeled interface that catches what an unlabeled one buries.
beats.visual_intent: Validation report: input '18.9 lbf*s', WARNING: converted via lbf_to_N factor 4.44822, output '84.0 N*s'

beats.narration_text: The Mars Climate Orbiter failure was not an arithmetic error — the individual calculations were correct. It was an interface error: two correct systems with incompatible assumptions at the boundary. A DimensionalValue type makes the unit part of the value, not a comment in the code.
beats.visual_intent: recap card: 'Unlabeled float: 18.9 (silently wrong) · DimensionalValue(18.9, lbf*s): caught at compile time'

beats.narration_text: Your move: find every floating-point value in your own codebase that represents a physical quantity. Wrap it in a DimensionalValue. The first UnitMismatchError you catch is probably a real bug that's been hiding.
beats.visual_intent: next-steps card: 1) audit float values for physical meaning, 2) wrap in DimensionalValue, 3) run the type system and find the hidden bugs

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI. Then take it apart. At Nik Bear Brown, nikbearbrown dot com.
